steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Label the side pair for the named angle first: opposite, adjacent, and hypotenuse. Then pick the matching shortcut, such as SOH/CAH/TOA, complementary trig values, or a special right-triangle ratio.

steps.title: Identify the complementary angle pair
steps.content: Shortcut (sin/cos): In a right triangle, the two acute angles always add to 90°, making them complementary. In right triangle MNP, angles M and N satisfy $M + N = 90^{\circ}$.

steps.title: Apply the co-function identity directly
steps.content: When two angles are complementary, $\sin(M) = \cos(N)$. We're given $\sin(M) = \dfrac{\sqrt{5}}{3}$, so $\cos(N) = \dfrac{\sqrt{5}}{3}$. That confirms B is correct.
